name: Pecan Pancake with Grilled Shrimp and Cucumber Salsa
recipeInstructions:
1. For shrimp, mix all ingredients in a medium glass bowl. Refrigerate, covered, for at least one hour.
2. For roasted red pepper sauce:
- 1 small red bell pepper, roasted (peeled and seeded)
- 1 cup Fish stock or clam juice
- 1/2 tsp Minced garlic
- 1/2 tsp Paprika
- 1/8 tsp Salt
- 1/8 tsp Ground cumin
- 1/8 tsp Oregano
- 1/8 tsp White pepper
- 1/4 cup Whipping cream
3. In a small non-aluminum saucepan, combine all ingredients except the whipping cream. Simmer for 20 minutes. Add the cream and simmer an additional 10 minutes. Puree in a blender or food processor until smooth. Taste and adjust seasonings. Keep warm.
4. For cucumber salsa:
- 1 small cucumber, peeled and seeded
- 1 plum tomato, finely diced
- 1 green onion, chopped
- 1 tbsp Minced cilantro
- 1 tbsp Sour cream
- 1/2 tsp Fresh lime juice
- 1/4 tsp Minced garlic
- 1/4 tsp Minced jalapeno
- 1/4 tsp Salt
- 1/8 tsp Ground cumin
- 1/8 tsp White pepper
5. For pecan pancakes:
- 6 tbsp Milk
- 1 Egg, beaten
- 1 tbsp Melted butter
- 1 medium red potato, peeled and grated
- 1/2 cup Finely chopped pecans
- 1/4 cup Minced onion
- 3/4 tsp Cornstarch
- 3/4 tsp Baking powder
- 1/2 tsp Minced garlic
- Salt and White pepper to taste
6. Heat oil in a large non-stick skillet. Pour one-fifth of the batter into the skillet to make a 4-inch-diameter pancake. Fry until golden and crisp on both sides, about 3 minutes. Repeat with remaining batter. Keep warm.
7. While pancakes cook, heat broiler or charcoal grill. Arrange shrimp on broiler pan or grill rack, about 6 inches from the heat. Cook, turning once, until pink and cooked through, about 4 minutes.
8. To serve, place one pancake on each serving plate. Top with cucumber salsa. Arrange four shrimp on top. Drizzle with warm red pepper sauce and garnish with cilantro sprigs.
Note: To roast a red pepper, place it directly over the gas flame or on a broiler pan 4 inches from the heat source. Roast, turning often, until the skin blackens and blisters on all sides, about 10 minutes. Wrap in foil and let stand for 10 minutes. Use a paring knife to remove the charred skin. Cut out the core and seeds.
